page: Amazon Web Services g5g.16xlarge is an ARM64-based server instance powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For graphics and parallel computing acceleration, the instance features two NVIDIA Turing T4G GPUs with a combined 32 GB of VRAM. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. No local NVMe storage is bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance characteristics for the ARM-based architecture. The instance demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 97,056.83 and a strong software compilation score of 42,664.0 in Geekbench. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong at 3,779.8 GB/sec, though large block read and write operations fall into the average tier. Single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with weak ratings in Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression tests. Database and web serving capabilities are strong, with Redis SET operations reaching 76.54 million operations per second and static web serving throughput measured at 44.64 GB/sec.

Large language model inference benchmarks show mixed results. Prompt processing for small and medium models is average, but text generation for small models is poor at 19.9 tokens per second. Large 70B model inference remains weak for both prompt processing and text generation. Consequently, this instance is suited for workloads such as graphics-intensive applications, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and GPU-accelerated ARM workloads, rather than heavy single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services g5g.16xlarge is an ARM64 server instance featuring 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 cores running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory and two NVIDIA Turing T4G GPUs providing 32 GB of VRAM. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network speed of 25 Gbps without local storage. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth for small cached blocks, alongside strong Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. However, single-core processing and single-threaded compression are weak. LLM inference performance is average for prompt processing on small to medium models but poor to weak for text generation and large models. This instance is suited for multi-threaded ARM applications, graphics-intensive tasks, and web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.16xlarge is an ARM64 server with 64 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA T4G GPUs. It delivers strong multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core speeds.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.16xlarge features 64 ARM64 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA T4G GPUs for graphics-intensive and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 architecture server combining sixty-four dedicated cores with dual graphics accelerators for multi-threaded and GPU-accelerated workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz.
Dual NVIDIA Turing T4G GPUs with 32 GB of total VRAM.
128.0 GB of system memory and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high static web serving throughput.
Weak single-core CPU speeds and single-threaded compression performance.
Suited for graphics-intensive applications, multi-threaded compilation, and Redis databases.
